Petition Concerning Internal Affairs Of Trust
Off Calendar Nov-30-2020 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Confirm Trust Assets
Off Calendar Sep-21-2020 Off Calendar

Petition Concerning Internal Affairs Of Trust
Off Calendar Sep-21-2020 Continued To Nov-30-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Diamond appeared by zoom for Nannette Eaton (Petitioner).
Brett Gibbs appeared by zoom for and with Christine McMillan (Successor Trustee) present in Court.
Larry Swift (Beneficiary/Interested Party) appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that its concern is that the cats and the cats' Guardian not be moved out of the condo while this matter is pending. Ms. McMillan stated that she agrees and that she will not take any action. The Court orders the Trustee not to pursue eviction of the cats or the Guardian until further order of this Court. Mr. Gibbs stated that the Trustee (Janesta Downey) resigned, that they appointed the second Trustee in line (Christine McMillan), referred to page 9 section 3 of the trust, and stated that they have been in settlement talks, but as of late Friday it appears settlement is not going to happen and requested a 60-day continuance. Mr. Diamond stated that the petition was filed nearly 15-months ago, that Ms. Downey was the Trustee up until last Friday, that Ms. Downey never filed a response to his petition and that the request for the approval of the settlement was denied. Mr. Diamond also stated that they were back in settlement discussion regarding having a Professional Fiduciary, that the talks broke down last week, that there is a lot of gamesmanship and that the Court still has jurisdiction. In addition, Mr. Diamond stated that he never spoke with Mr. Gibbs, that he needs clarification as to who Mr. Gibbs represents, that the Court should rule that his Client is the proper caretaker of the cats as wished by the Decedent, that he does not know what the game is and asked the Court to order removal of both of the Trustees. Furthermore, Mr. Diamond stated that Ms. Downey should be ordered to present her accounting to the Court and that the Court should order that the unlawful in detainer Alameda be dismissed. The Court stated that it did not see any documents regarding nomination of an independent Trustee. Mr. Diamond stated that he did not proceed because they were in settlement talks. Mr. Gibbs stated that the resignation resolves some of the conflict of interest with Ms. Downey, that it is his understanding that the Weisberg & Miller firm represent Ms. McMillan and that currently he does not know why the unlawful detainer should be dismissed. The Court noted that it was told that an opposition to Mr. Diamond's petition would be filed. Mr. Gibbs stated that they are in the midst of settlement, that the issue of removal of the Trustee has been done and requested a 60-day continuance. Ms. McMillan stated that she is not a Beneficiary, that she was a good friend of Liza and that she wants to act in the most fair and legal way, that she needs time to get caught up, that she has a CPA working on the accounting and that Liza chose her as the Successor Trustee. Mr. Diamond asked for a referral to mediation. Mr. Gibbs stated that he has no issue with that. Mr. Swift stated that he is fine with mediation and that he prefers to have a Professional Fiduciary. The Parties indicated that they would like to go back to Bette Epstein. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than November 16, 2020. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner and file a report as to the outcome of mediation by November 23, 2020. The Petition Concerning Internal Affairs of Trust is continued to and the Parties are to return on November 30, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. If mediation is unsuccessful, an opposition is to be filed by November 20, 2020.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Helen Trowbridge. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition Concerning Internal Affairs Of Trust
Off Calendar Aug-13-2020 Continued To Sep-21-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition Concerning Internal Affairs Of Trust
Off Calendar Jul-22-2020 Continued To Aug-17-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Jul-22-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances; Dave Diamond appeared by zoom for Nannette Eaton (Beneficiary).
Craig Miller appeared by zoom for Janesta Downey (Successor Trustee).
Larry Swift appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that it read the opposition that was filed yesterday. Mr. Diamond and Mr. Miller indicated that they had not seen the opposition. The Court stated that it does not see how this settlement agreement is consistent with what Liza wanted to do with this trust, Mr. Miller stated that Liza did not know that by having the cats live in the house it would keep the Beneficiaries from having their bequeath, spoke of the possibility of the cats living for several years, stated that the cats are going to go with Ms. Eaton and just not be living in the house, and spoke of holding up of the gifts and the draining of the estate. Mr. Diamond stated that his Client lived with the Decedent prior to the Decedent's demise, that his Client had lengthy conversations with the Decedent, that what the Decedent really wanted was that the cats stay with his Client and that Liza did not know that there would be litigation. Mr. Diamond also stated that it was not a well thought out plan, and that the settlement agreement is designed to achieve the intent that the cats are taken care of and that the Beneficiaries receive the gifts they are entitled to. Mr. Swift stated that he agrees with the Court, that the trust should be followed to a tee, spoke of the unlawful detainer action against Ms. Eaton, stated that he wants to see that his Sister's wishes are followed to a tee and that the settlement agreement should not be allowed. Mr. Diamond stated that he is not sure that Mr. Swift has any standing, that the settlement does not impact Mr. Swift economically either way, that one cat is 10 or 11, that the other cat is about 5 years old, that Ms. Eaton is taking care of the cats and has been living with the cats for five years, and that Ms. Eaton considers them her cats. Mr. Miller stated that the settlement agreement effectuates as close as possible what Liza would have wanted. The Court stated that it is not convinced that it is appropriate to approve the settlement agreement, that it does not think that the settlement agreement adheres to the plain language of the trust and that it is not prepared to grant this. The Court also raised the issue of appointment of a neutral temporary trustee, and stated that it will not do that sua sponte and that it is concerned about how the trust is being read. Mr. Miller stated that he is not prepared to address the 17200 petition and asked for time to brief it. The Court denied the Petition to Approve Settlement Agreement finding that it is in conflict with the terms of the trust and declined the request to modify the trust based on PC 15409. The Petition Concerning Internal Affairs of Trust that is currently set for July 27, 2020 is continued to August 17, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Miller assured Mr. Diamond that he will not act on the unlawfull detainer action in Alamdea County until after the next hearing.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

Petition Concerning Internal Affairs Of Trust
Off Calendar Dec-02-2019 Continued To Feb-10-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-19-302978 The Liza Denise Swift Living Trust and
PTR-19-303281 The Liza Denise Swift Living Trust.

Appearances: David Diamond appeared by telephone for Nannette Eaton.
Jeanne Grove for and with Larry Swift (Beneficiary/Objector).
William Weisberg for Janesta Downey (Trustee).

Ms. Grove stated that she does not know if the case was transferred over from the Alameda Court and that she will work on getting all of the pleadings transferred here. Mr. Weisberg stated that the trust is possibly worth over $3,000,000.00. The Court informed Counsel that they need to file an ex parte petition to consolidate both matters and they need to provide hard courtesy copies of all of the documents from Alameda to this Court. Mr. Weisberg stated that there are four matters (Alameda Hegstad, Mr. Diamonds petition, partition action here in Civil, unlawful detainer in Alameda) that are intertwined and that they plan on mediating with Ms. Epstein. Ms. Grove stated that the Balboa property is in dispute and that if it is found that the trust does not own 50% of it, the value of the trust will be below$3,000,000.00. Mr. Diamond asked to use the Court's Pro Bono Mediation program stating that his Client cannot bear the cost of mediation. Discussion was held with regard to stay of the unlawful detainer. Ms. Weisberg clarified that the stay of discovery with regard to her matters. Mr. Diamond and Mr. Weisberg agreed to participate in mediation with respect to the petition concerning internal affairs. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than January 17, 2020. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by January 24, 2020. The Petition Concerning Internal Affairs of Trust is continued to and the Parties are to return on February 10, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The Petition for Construction of Trust, etc. and the Petition for Confirm Trust Assets is also continued to February 10, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Trustee is to respond to Mr. Diamond's petition by January 24, 2020. The ex parte petition to consolidate is to be filed and copies of the Alameda documents are to be provided by December 16, 2019.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
